item.title: What Both Anthropic and the Pentagon Get Wrong
item.description: Neither Anthropic nor the Pentagon are thinking about this issue in a meaningful way.

item.title: The Best-Kept Secret in Washington
item.description: On what makes Congress secret and toxic.

item.title: Here’s How Mamdani Can Reduce Homelessness
item.description: Our aim should be to make permanent housing, not shelters, the default.

item.title: You Can’t Shame People Into Vaccinating Their Kids
item.description: For a long time, I was angry at my mom for not vaccinating me.

item.title: Trump’s ‘Two Minutes Hate’ Won’t Pay the Bills
item.description: The State of the Union was full of fearmongering. That strategy isn’t working with voters anymore — and Donald Trump is handing Democrats a gift.

item.title: Trump’s Shields Are Down
item.description: Personnel once meant policy; now it means flattery.

item.title: Donald Trump Is the Crypto President. Why Is It Struggling?
item.description: Ryan Cummings and Jared Bernstein argue that the industry is struggling because its core product is basically useless.

item.title: ‘We’re Going to the Moon and Mars’
item.description: The new head of NASA predicts a manned mission to Mars in 10 years.

item.title: What Does NASA Know About Aliens?
item.description: Does NASA have files on aliens? Jared Isaacman says no, and tells Ross Douthat on “Interesting Times” that extraterrestrials would pick a more exciting locale to visit than America’s naval bases.

item.title: First the Moon, Then Mars
item.description: The NASA administrator Jared Isaacman tells Ross Douthat how his trips to space shaped his perspective on space travel.

item.title: Trump’s State of the Union Was a Win for Democrats
item.description: Ezra Klein and Aaron Retica react to Trump’s 2026 State of the Union speech.

item.title: The World Is Still Converting to Clean Energy
item.description: It feels as if our political institutions have abandoned the climate, but the clean energy economy is still growing.

item.title: Sanae Takaichi’s Rise Changes the Game for China
item.description: Sanae Takaichi’s political rise presages a stronger Japan that could reshape the strategic balance in Asia and help deter Chinese aggression.

item.title: ‘Time Is Not on Russia’s Side’: A Q & A With Michael Kofman
item.description: If the battle for Ukraine Is not World War I or World War III, what is it?

item.title: Mexico’s Cartel Fight Is a Terrifying High-Wire Act
item.description: As drug gangs have become more deeply embedded in Mexico, narco blockades have become a way for them to visibly assert their power.

item.title: The Incompetence of Trump 2.0
item.description: President Trump’s top officials are often portrayed as cunning political strategists. But too often, it’’s their carelessness and incompetence that put Americans at risk — and citizens are paying the price, argues Times Opinion’s editor, Kathleen Kingsbury.

item.title: How to Measure Real Progress in Education
item.description: Randi Weingarten, president of the American Federation of Teachers, and others respond to a Nicholas Kristof column. Also: Reagan’s warning about nuclear weapons; aging doctors.

item.title: The Trumpiest Trump We’ve Ever Beheld
item.description: The president is becoming the very thing that destroyed the Democrats: a reality-denying machine.

item.title: I’m a Mathematician. Trump’s Fun With Numbers Is Embarrassing.
item.description: The Trump administration is creative when it comes to numbers.

item.title: Trump’s State of the Union Was Rage Bait
item.description: President Trump’s State of the Union address leaned heavily on rage bait for one reason, the Times Opinion columnist Jamelle Bouie argues: The president is trying to distract from the fact that he is failing to govern the country effectively.

item.title: The New Space Race
item.description: Can NASA and Elon Musk get us to Mars and beyond?

item.title: These Iranian Doctors Risked Their Lives So You Could See These Images
item.description: The Iranian regime massacred thousands of its own citizens when protests swept the country last month. As injured and dying protesters flooded hospitals, medical workers began smuggling evidence of that violence and brutality out to the rest of the world, despite the risk to their own lives.

item.title: I Thought I Understood A.I. Companies. I Couldn’t Have Been More Wrong.
item.description: The future of the field is still up for grabs.

item.title: The Uncomfortable Experience of Watching ‘America’s Next Top Model’ 20 Years Later
item.description: What’s worse for body image — the fashion industry or the algorithm?

item.title: Best and Worst Moments From Trump’s State of the Union Address
item.description: Eight Opinion writers weigh in on the speech.

item.title: America Has an Edge Over China. Why Won’t We Use It?
item.description: American manufacturing prowess won’t be restored without bringing new technologies to the factory floor.

item.title: What Hath Trump Wrought
item.description: The real state of the union.

item.title: How Did Trump’s State of the Union Play?
item.description: Readers respond to the president’s address. Most but not all are critical.

item.title: Images the Iranian Regime Doesn’t Want You to See
item.description: These X-rays show evidence of the Iranian regime’s massacre.

item.title: The Great Capitulation Is Over. What Will Take Its Place?
item.description: Appeasing a predatory hegemon doesn’t work.

item.title: How ‘Bridgerton’ Lost Its Way
item.description: Performative progressiveness feels like a fake orgasm: Everybody knows it’s not sincere and no one is happy.

item.title: These Iranian Doctors Risked Their Lives So You Could See These Images
item.description: These X-rays show evidence of the Iranian regime’s massacre.

item.title: America Might Not Rule, but the Dollar Still Does
item.description: The currency is stumbling, by many accounts. But the narrative is false.

item.title: How Fast Will A.I. Agents Rip Through the Economy?
item.description: The Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark tells Ezra Klein what he sees coming in the new era of A.I. agents.

item.title: John Roberts Is Losing Patience With Trump
item.description: How to read the gratuitous paragraph in the chief justice’s tariff opinion.

item.title: Whatever Happened to Grown-Up Movies for Kids?
item.description: We need stories that are more adult than Y.A. and less explicit than HBO.

item.title: The Donald Trump 1 Percent Fan Club
item.description: There are some elites the president likes.

item.title: Winter Is One of the Last Threads Holding Everything in Place
item.description: We are only at the beginning of climate destabilization, in which winter weather grows more erratic and extreme.

item.title: At Risk: Afghan Allies and America’s Credibility
item.description: Readers discuss the end of visas for Afghan wartime partners. Also: Timely election results; Russian sexual violence in Ukraine; cash for the 250th anniversary.

item.title: Trump’s New Tariffs Are Illegal Too
item.description: Just another attempt to ignore the law and dare the courts to step in.

item.title: After a Big Loss, What to Expect From Trump at the State of the Union
item.description: The Supreme Court’s tariffs ruling could make for a tense night.

item.title: Trump Is Turning the F.C.C. and F.T.C. Against Free Expression
item.description: Trump appointees have transformed the F.T.C. and F.C.C. into instruments of ideological enforcement.
